message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding per-entry TTL support to DSPy cache layers (memory+disk)

type: INTENT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding ttl param to Cache.__init__/configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE, TTLCache import, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() in get() miss path, plus set_ttl() helper exported in __all__ - will keep edits additive/minimal to reduce merge conflicts

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression implemented and tested: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache, added Cache.set_compression(), _encode_disk_value()/_decode_disk_value() helpers, put()/get() now route disk bytes through them; edits are additive, only touched __init__ signature, put/get bodies, and added new methods+constants - should merge cleanly with additive TTL params as long as we both add new kwargs rather than reordering existing ones

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: TTL feature complete and tested (memory TTLCache swap, disk_cache.set expire=ttl, expire() lazy purge, set_ttl helper); edits additive only (new ttl kwarg appended last in Cache.__init__/configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE, new set_ttl function, __all__ append) - should merge cleanly with your compression kwargs
